name: Coll Formic to Viladrau – return through the Montseny Natural Park
name_original: Coll Formic hasta Viladrau  – vuelta por el Parque Natural del Montseny
region_intro: Road cycling around Viladrau offers diverse terrain within the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features mostly paved surfaces, ranging from gentle inclines to challenging ascents through varied landscapes. Cyclists can navigate lush forests of chestnut, beech, and fir trees, transitioning to high mountain meadows. The area is also characterized by numerous natural springs and rivers, contributing to its rich ecological environment. Best road cycling routes around Viladrau The most popular road cycling route is Collsesplanes and Sant Sadurní d'Osormort Loop, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This route offers a balanced ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll Formic to Viladrau – return through the Montseny Natural Park, a difficult 27.2 miles (43.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it traverses the protected natural park. Local road cyclists also love the Osor Road – Els Nínxols of Osor loop from Viladrau, a 58.3 miles (93.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Viladrau is defined by forested areas, mountain passes, and scenic roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Viladrau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 12,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Viladrau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Viladrau?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Viladrau, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Viladrau?
A: Yes, Viladrau offers routes suitable for various fitness levels. While many routes feature significant elevation gain, there are 4 easy and 23 moderate routes available. These provide a good starting point for less experienced riders or those looking for a more relaxed ride through the beautiful Montseny landscape.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in Viladrau?
A: Road cycling in Viladrau offers spectacular scenery within the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You'll traverse lush forests of chestnut, beech, and fir trees, transition to high mountain meadows, and encounter numerous natural springs and rivers. The region is renowned for its diverse ecosystem and breathtaking views.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Viladrau is an excellent destination for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. There are 23 difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995637581/coll-formic-to-viladrau-return-through-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic to Viladrau – return through the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain over its 27.2 miles (43.7 km). Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33990143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osor Road – Els Nínxols of Osor loop from Viladrau&lt;/a&gt;, a 58.3 miles (93.8 km) trail often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can find panoramic views from the Collformic road, especially near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/582302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m)&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable points include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordoriol Pass&lt;/a&gt; and various ancient bridges like Pont de Can Pau Moliner, which are part of local trails.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or natural springs?
A: Yes, Viladrau is known for its abundant water features, with over 200 fountains and numerous springs, rivers, and waterfalls. While specific road cycling routes might not directly lead to all of them, the region's 'fountain route' is a popular option for exploring many natural springs. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2757495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Sort&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful waterfall in the area.

Q: Are there loop routes available for road cyclists?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Viladrau are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995638792/collsesplanes-and-sant-sadurni-d-osormort-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsesplanes and Sant Sadurní d'Osormort Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 19.0 miles (30.6 km) ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39549369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll del Buc (728 m) – Viladrau loop from Taradell&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Viladrau?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. Cyclists often praise the spectacular scenery, the quality of the mostly paved roads, and the diverse terrain that caters to various fitness levels. The combination of challenging climbs and scenic descents through protected natural environments is a common highlight.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes on every route are not guaranteed, the region has charming villages and local establishments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Te Restaurant & Bar&lt;/a&gt; is located near a mountain pass, offering a potential stop for refreshments. Exploring nearby villages like Espinelves also provides opportunities for breaks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Viladrau?
A: The Montseny region experiences varied weather conditions due to its diverse ecosystem. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery with lush foliage or vibrant autumn colors, making them ideal for road cycling. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while higher passes might experience cooler temperatures. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before your ride.

Q: Are there any other interesting attractions near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, Viladrau offers unique attractions. You can explore the Arborètum de Bruixes i Bandolers, an open-air interpretive garden combining Montseny's vegetation with local legends. The area is also close to other charming villages like Espinelves, which can be incorporated into longer scenic routes.
tips: Small town with special architecture. You can refill the water in the square.
Town in the region of Osona, located right on the border with the natural park of Montseny.

Parking area with capacity for 20 vehicles. 500 meters from this point, a new parking area with capacity for 40 more vehicles.

Information point on Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays from 10 am to 3 pm.

Start of itineraries:
GR 5-2a Coll Formic - Matagalls
SL-C 85 From Brull to Collformic
GR 5.2 From Coffee to Collformic

A beautiful point to rest and appreciate the views of the Collformic.

On days with good visibility you can also see the Pyrenees.

Long climb with spectacular views on the southern side. And a good amount of forest which provides a bit of cover from the sun during the summer.

Towards the upper part, it is possible to turn off on a small road, to connect other side, or continue up to Turó do l'Home.

I prefer descenting on the southern side, so that you really get to enjoy the views.
Great place to fill up the bidons, always cool and refreshing!

Viladrau is a municipality in the province of Gerona, Catalonia, Spain. It is located in the region of Osona, to the east, on the border with the regions of La Selva and Vallés Oriental.

Viladrau is known nationally for the natural mineral water that bears the name of the same village.
